page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is a memory-configured server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs derived from 24 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8581C processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 372 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 7.75 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong computational and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by a Geekbench single-core score of 2208.0 and a bzip3 single-threaded decompression rate of 14.54 GB/sec. Multi-threaded capabilities are also robust, with a Passmark CPU score of 57593.71 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 17983.0. Advanced vector and matrix extensions, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX, are present to support accelerated workloads such as LLM inference, where it processes 2496.82 tokens/sec for a small 135M model. However, text generation on larger models like the 70B parameter model drops to 2.74 tokens/sec due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-highmem-48 offers a dense memory profile relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable alternative to fully dedicated memory-optimized instances for workloads that require substantial memory but do not scale linearly with CPU cores. This resource balance is highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, including database operations, Redis in-memory caching, software compilation, and medium-scale data processing.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is an x86_64 server instance configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 372 GB of RAM, delivering 7.75 GB of memory per core. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz, it features 24 physical cores and supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show strong performance across diverse workloads, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2208.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 57593.71. It excels in memory-bound tasks, demonstrating high throughput in Redis SET operations and database operations. This configuration is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, in-memory caching, and software compilation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is an x86_64 server featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 372 GB of RAM. It delivers strong database and memory performance without local storage or GPUs.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-48 is an x86_64 server with 48 vCPUs and 372 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive workloads and databases.
tagline: A high-density memory instance featuring 48 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, optimized for database operations, caching, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 24 physical cores.
Equipped with 372 GB of RAM, providing 7.75 GB of memory per core.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix operations.
Delivers 58.32 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmark tests.
Configured without local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.
Best suited for database hosting, in-memory caching, and software compilation.
